text: When you start working with APIs, you will likely use HTTP requests to communicate with a web server. Most APIs return data in JSON format, which is easy to read and work with in many programming languages. Some APIs require authentication, often using an API key or token, to ensure only authorized users can access the data. You should also be aware of rate limits, which restrict how many requests you can make in a certain period. Finally, always handle errors properly by checking response status codes; for example, a 404 means the resource is not found, while a 500 indicates a server issue.
